context.description: A philosophical prose passage defines consciousness as distinct from attention and contrasts outward and inward attention with a more universal, non-targeted awareness. It introduces the idea of "two-track awareness" and argues that pure awareness can encompass both the speaker and the surrounding context.
normalization_notes: Translated German prose and heading into English control-language summary.
No chapter number or title was explicitly visible beyond the section heading.

rows.text_content: Defines consciousness as broader than attention and distinguishes outward and inward attention from a more universal awareness.

rows.paragraph_text: Awareness can encompass a conversation, the speaker, and the surrounding environment without being narrowed to a single target.
rows.text_content: Pure awareness can include the conversation, the speaker, and the surrounding environment.

rows.paragraph_text: Outward attention and inward attention are contrasted, and the text argues that pure awareness is not limited by goals or distracting thoughts.
rows.text_content: Outward and inward attention are contrasted with pure awareness that is not limited by goals or distracting thoughts.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy philosophy_of_consciousness Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2 on consciousness and awareness. Section heading: Two-Track Awareness. The passage contrasts attention with broader awareness and describes pure awareness as encompassing both the conversation and its context.
